key_insight: GMHF uses expert feedback to steer a generative model toward the unseen target distribution, improving meta-learning generalization under distribution shift.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and other high-stakes engineering settings, the framework offers a practical way to inject domain expertise when target-domain data are scarce or unavailable.
method: Theoretical generalization bounds motivate minimizing divergence between generated data and human-believed target physics.
A Conditional Neural ODE serves as a generative digital twin to synthesize trajectories.
A reinforcement learning agent updates latent physical parameters using expert feedback to align generated samples with the target domain.
evidence_signal: On a nonlinear Duffing oscillator, deployment loss decreased as expert reliability increased, and generated-target divergence dropped under reliable feedback; additional probabilistic-model experiments showed the approach generalizes beyond ODE systems.
watch_item: The method depends on the quality and reliability of human feedback, so weak or biased expert input could misguide the generator.
paper_title: Human-Machine Collaboration on Generative Meta-Learning: Model and Algorithm
